description_long: This object is a large, roughly rectangular stone block with a prominent, fossil-like spiral structure embedded on its top surface. The stone appears to be weathered, with visible striations and a slightly uneven, matte finish. The block is mounted on a squared pedestal base, which is smoother and more regular in shape compared to the organic, textured top. One side of the pedestal features a plaque, suggesting it may serve as a display or commemorative marker. The coloration is predominantly gray with some brownish and off-white patches, especially around the fossil feature. The overall appearance suggests a blend of natural geological form and human intervention, likely intended for public display in an urban or park setting.
description: A large, weathered stone block with a fossil-like spiral on top, mounted on a squared pedestal with a plaque. The surface is textured and gray, with some brown and off-white areas.
description_view_0: The fossil spiral is most prominent, with a rough, chipped area visible on the left side of the top surface.
description_view_1: The front view shows the plaque on the pedestal, with the fossil spiral receding slightly to the left.
description_view_2: The spiral is centered and the block appears more uniform, with less visible detail on the pedestal.
description_view_3: The fossil spiral is seen from an angle, with a brownish patch on the right edge and the pedestal appearing slightly darker.
attribute_location_context: public park or urban plaza as a commemorative or educational display
attribute_installation_method: set on a prepared concrete or stone foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of weathering with some discoloration and minor chipping, especially on the fossil feature. The finish is matte and slightly rough, indicating exposure to the elements.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: A plaque with inscriptions is visible on the pedestal; no other engravings are apparent.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely intended as a standalone display or monument
